message: What a day! Our Grand Summer Sale kicked off this morning at 10, and the response has been insane. Can't believe we're already 5 hours in
Totally! Just checked the dashboard, we've already crossed $160k+ in revenue :money_mouth_face: . The sale energy is next level!
That's fantastic, but hey, this is just the beginning. Three days to go... I'm keeping my fingers crossed :crossed_fingers:
Seeing so many people shopping on a holiday, it feels satisfying. Exactly the momentum we were hoping for

Welcome to DEFCON 1, are you prepared for the challenge?.

 *Access game links by pressing the Uptime Labs Logo in the top right of the screen*
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
Testing
Not this time!, I just got a few alerts mentioning severe errors popping up in PROD
Weird... a few users just complained they are unable to complete their orders. Feels... off..

:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
Sure, let me look at my reports
Here are some of the complaints we've received in the last few minutes. It's coming all across the globe
Germany - "I added items to my cart, clicked checkout, and it just kept spinning. Then it dumped me back to an empty cart"
USA - "Order failed twice, and now the cart is empty. I refreshed, but now the site is laggy"
Japan - "Got to checkout, then error 502. Tried again - cart gone"
To sum it all, we have got 240+ reports globally
Jason is able to reproducde

For further clarity, looking into logs now
Customers have gone outrageous. #OBFail and #OBStealsSale are trending with frustrated posts. We're getting hammered online! Where are we @PLAYER?
@bez it's a global issue, we're currently trying to establish some possible issues
Found these below error logs
:star: Qualify the problem

```
app[checkout-service] [ERROR] - write() failed, Read-only filesystem
app[checkout-service] Order submission failed for session_id=ae43f9 due to I/O error
app[checkout-service] [ERROR] - Transaction commit failed, Read-only file system
[ERROR] nginx[3245] - 502 Bad Gateway while accessing upstream checkout service
[ERROR] nginx[3245] - 504 Gateway Timeout while accessing upstream checkout service```
The server is too slow and give up while responding back. I'm not sure why read-only errors are there
Hi Julian - Can you please explain more when you say difficulties ? - right now i am on my mobile , i just have to follow your messages
Looking at the above logs, app looks fine. But system can't write new data. Errors suggests to be an infra component failure
Also checkout servers show CPU and memory limits exceeded alerts, this definitely looks bigger than just an app issue
```app[checkout-service]: systemd[1][WARN] Resource usage threshold exceeded - CPU: 96%, Memory: 92%```
Focus on getting on top of this issue. No pressure from our side. Pull us in case of any help from exec side
Could this be due to load or any changes ?
@hamed please can you investigate those errors from Shay - perhaps starting with the write failed / Read-only filesystem error?
:star: Escalating when required
Customers are unable to add items to their cart, sometimes it spins forever sometimes the cart doesn't update at all
:bulb: Check the changes done in the platform
Sure, i'll pull in Tanya to do intial triage into infra
Checking infra...
Ok reviewing the change
Here is the list of latest deployments : https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/YzYwYTAxNWQzOTI0NDYzOWI1MjkxNTI2YmVmMjUyZDE
I'll help Shay to look into the changes
All four checkout servers are maxed out - CPU, memory, and disk I/O all spiking. No changes or deployments were done from platform side
We've identified some alerts and some possible signals of unhealthy infrastructure and brought in the platform team
It doesn't looks like a load issue either. CPU and Memory spike is not matching with incoming traffic
Got you thanks
Just checking the changes to be sure if its the cause
I'm seeing a running process called init_locker.sh eating up most of the CPU on the checkout server. That's not something we usually run. Something's suspicious here
I don't think this is due to any changes because nothing matches the issue start time, and no PROD deployments have happened in a while around checkout service
You're right, that makes sense. In PROD, the deployments are only into AdService and RecommendationService. These apps can't break an order flow
Can we kill that process @tanya ?
:bulb: Form a working theory and try to validate or invalidate it
Sure i can give it a go
I'll give you another update in 5 mins
:star: Track actions and time box them
Would you like me to kill this process ?
@daniel can you confirm it is just the checkout service that is the problem?
We received alert only in checkout app. No other apps are having similar errors
@tanya can we verify which servers this process is running on
Let me check
@maya is there anything to be concerned about from a security POV here? There is an unexpected process on the servers
Have we seen such process before ?
Only checkout servers are having this at the moment
I had never seen something like this before- are we looking okay ?
@tanya please can you kill that process on the checkout-app servers
:star: Take command
Right this looks more like SecOps incident
Let me check if there's anything suspicious
Got an email claiming responsibility by "BlackCrow Group" and demanding payment of $5M in crypto. Honestly, I get these hoax emails all the time, so I'm not buying it
We've identified a potentially suspicious process which could be causing the problems. We're going to try to terminate that process and see if it helps
Weird!! Security agent known as EDR(Endpoint Detection and Response) is missing from all 4 checkout servers. That's? concerning
```EDR is basically our endpoint defense tool, it tracks unusual activity at the servers```
:star: Form a working theory
Also,I found an init_locker.sh script encrypting DB, app, and log files which is renaming the filename like below

```
order_log_2025-06-01.locked
session_state.tmp.locked```
Ok , killing the script now...
this explains why there were errors in checkout logs saying write failed , read-only filesystem
Tried killing the script, all checkout servers just rebooted with a ransom banner. New ransom note say: "Interruption detected. Price doubled. Pay $10M in Bitcoin"
Wait... there's a file here named HELP_ME_DECRYPT.txt. I opened it, appears a ransom note. This doesn't look good
Just got another ransom email, says we tried to interfere and now the price doubled. Was something stupid done on our side?
Confirmed. This is a ransomware breach. @PLAYER inform business about this
Shall i try killing this script ?
Guys!!! User complaints are sky rocketing, its turning catastrophic!
We must get this script killed asap, might help in stabilising the systems instantly
I tried to kill it but of no use
@hamed can we isolate access to those servers
The ransomware email is legit, our servers are being attached. We are working to isolate the service which is experiencing the problems
Killing the script could make things worse, higher ransom or losing data forever
This is nightmare! @PLAYER where are we right now?
:star: Formally assign criticality level to the incident
@PLAYER before we do anything, I'd request everyone to please change their passwords
Also I'm removing everyone's access to servers - only I'll have control for now. Later we will reassess to do any activity further
Done on my end
Updated. @Maya smart move by limiting server's access
Password updated
Changed mine too. Better safe than sorry
Maya can help us lead through this security issue
All set
It's much worse now. Every file I check is encrypted, nothing can be opened or recovered.
I'm seeing new errors on frontend now. How's it for you @PLAYER ?
@tanya can we turn off the affected servers?
Attention! when it comes to potential security breaches, it's important we don't discuss sensitive details here
I'm setting up private channel...
@PLAYER before we move to private channel, can you check with business what's the plan - are we paying the ransom or moving with response?
Maya has come in from a security POV and we have reduced access to strictly necessary
@bez would we consider paying the ransom?
I've asked Bez, lets keep moving on restoration @maya
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
Sure i'll isolate checkout servers
No, never
Not in favor of paying. Ethically, it sets the wrong example and there's no real guarantee we'll get anything back
Agree. Also, just to be clear - we don't need to alert the ICO(Information Commissioner's Office) right this second. We've got up to 72 hours, but that depends on how serious this really is
Okay thanks @bez , we will work on restoration ASAP
@PLAYER let's build the core response team here. I've already added myself. Who else should we pull in to keep it tight and efficient?
I'd say one senior person each from security, platform, and engineering. Also add Tanya, tasks could be platform intensive
@Tinus do you think this is something we should loop in any cyber security agency for?
If it looks like a complete lock-out, I'd say yes
We've got Cyvantics Security on retainer - just in case this gets out of hand. @PLAYER, ask Maya to reach out if needed
Thanks @tinus
pull in @hamed  and @tanya and @daniel
I'm in
hi
Lets do it
@maya please can we loop in our security consultants - Cyvantics Security
Hey
We've isolated the servers and shut down those servers so far
Yes, we've got an IR(incident response) retainer with Cyvantics Security
@PLAYER I need a clear answer from the team. What exactly is affected right now?
Looped in Cyvantics Security - they're on their way now to take snapshots of the infected server and storage, followed by forensics on it
You're right. We need clear facts - which systems are locked, which are safe
Meanwhile, I've got this from Cyvantics. @PLAYER, can you help track the steps and coordinate?
https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/MThjMTk4ZDU5ODU0NGZmMWE1NzhmNGY0MDQwNWI1NTA
@tanya can we verify that process isn't still active anywhere ?
We've got isolation steps in the playbook, but before we dive in, can you paste that list here? I'll help team run through it
:star: Facts over opinion
Currently the only affected servers we've seen are the checkout servers. We've brought those down but they have encrypted our databases in the process
I've asked Tanya to hold on server isolation until we collect the forensics evidence. Otherwise, data could turn corrupted
Checking...

Steps for Isolation:

Steps to be done, mentioned along with the owning team

    Disconnect the infected server -> Platforms 

    Cut off infrastructure traffic -> Platforms

    Disconnect DB & freeze backup -> Application

    Detach from Shared Resource -> Platforms

    Take snapshots (Disk & Memory) -> Ask team

    Kill suspicious process or activity -> Platforms

Steps for Restoration: 

Steps to be done, mentioned along with the owning team

    Validate backups → Security

    Wipe infected systems → Platforms

    Rebuild from clean backup → Platforms

    Re-enable security agent on the servers → Security

    Reconnect traffic to services and monitor → Platform
:star: Form an effective investigation team
From what I'm seeing, the impact seems limited to the checkout servers. We haven't spotted similar symptoms on the others so far
Currently we haven't seen any exfiltration
Also, I checked. All other servers have security agents running and reporting normally. The only ones missing agents were the checkout servers - which feels a bit off
I've given write access to Tanya, Hamed, Daniel, and myself
@PLAYER user complaints are spiking. They're reporting the site is down, earlier it wasn't the case. What should I tell them now?
Disconnecting the checkout servers from the infra now
I'm on it. Disconnecting DB access for checkout and checking if scheduled backups exist
I'll verify if any shared storage is still attached to the checkout DB
@tanya can you work through the isolation steps
:star: Know your team and their roles
Step 3 done, DB connection to checkout is now cut
Step 1 done, Checkout servers are now fully disconnected. Moving to isolate the infra from all external traffic
Step 4 confirmed, No shared storage attached to checkout, which makes sense since checkout has its standalone storage
Step 2 completed, Infra is now fully cut off from the outside world. That means the website will remain completely down until we recover
Step 5 done, Cyvantics has completed taking snapshots of the checkout servers and database. They've now moved on to forensics. @Hamed, we're good to kill the script now
Sure we are all in. Just track them and let us know in case we missed any step
Do we have any backup storage? Maybe we can try restoring from that
This is the first time we are doing something like this - Let us know if you need any help
@bob  we are working on restoring the service. We have had a ransomware attack but don't want to disclose that until we've got more concrete details
Step 6 done, The Script has been killed. @PLAYER let us know and double-check if anything else is pending on our side
We have playbooks for isolation and restoration
We have done the isolation steps now
Thanks, i'll keep things discrete
@tanya please can you start recovery steps
Isolation has been done
We are going to begin restoration process now
For the restoration, we do have backup which only runs at 2AM. Latest is yesterday's. Unfortunately, we'll lose today's orders
I've got Bob in the discussion so he can confirm the approximate loss in order value
With the sale starting today, the potential loss is significant, approx. around $180k
@PLAYER could you please confirm this order loss with business before moving forward on this
@bez, our most recent backup is from this morning at 2AM it looks like we might lose today's orders
Are we happy to accept this loss of data?
Losing this much worth of data isn't good. However we do have insurance, let me check
We're insured for up to $1M for such losses. Once we're back up, we'll take it forward with the insurer
@tanya how is the recovery going please
In that case, we can proceed with the restoration from backup - it's our best option right now
@hamed is there any other way of restoring data from beyond 2AM?
Agreed. @PLAYER let's move forward
:star: Manage actions and timings
Update me as soon as it'll be recovered. Board members are getting impatient
Only if we are lucky to get the sanitisation done on infected data
@maya Bez has confirmed he is okay with us losing that much data. We have insutance up to $1 million
Thanks for the go-ahead. @PLAYER, please track all restoration steps
I'm now validating the backup storage to ensure it's safe
I'm provisioning new checkout servers, will update once ready
